title: Reflecting Pool Contract Has ‘Inflated’ Profit Margin, Government Analysis Finds
description: The firm that was given a no-bid contract to fix the troubled landmark is charging 20 percent. The typical profit margin is 6 percent to 12 percent, internal records show.
author: David A. Fahrenthold

title: Biden Sues Justice Dept. to Block Release of Tapes
description: The former president argued that the Justice Department has a responsibility to protect the privacy of conversations he had with a former ghostwriter.
author: Karoun Demirjian

title: Alabama Republicans Ask Supreme Court to Allow New Congressional Map in Redistricting Fight
description: Republican leaders in the state have asked the justices to clear the way for a congressional map that a lower court found discriminated against Black voters.
author: Abbie VanSickle

title: Ball State Settles With Ex-Employee for $225,000 Over Charlie Kirk Posts
description: Ball State University is the latest institution to agree to pay workers who lost their jobs over their posts about the conservative activist.
author: Jeremy W. Peters and Sabrina Tavernise

title: Bo French, Anti-Muslim Candidate, Wins G.O.P. Runoff for Texas Oil Job
description: Bo French’s runoff victory gave Democrats fresh hope that they could compete for a seat regulating the state’s powerful oil and gas industry.
author: Lauren McGaughy

title: South Carolina Redistricting: Senate Passes on New Map, Defying Trump
description: With early voting already underway, it became clear there was no longer enough support to use new lines before the November midterm elections.
author: Eduardo Medina, Emily Cochrane and Nick Corasaniti

title: The Two Issues That Could Swing This District
description: Two national controversies — A.I. data centers and ICE detention centers — are converging in one swing congressional district. Our Southwest reporter Reis Thebault visits the town of Marana, Ariz., where some people are angry about both potential projects.
author: Reis Thebault, Christina Shaman, Anna Clare Spelman and June Kim

title: Two Survivors Left at Sea After U.S. Attacks Boat in Pacific
description: One person was killed in the U.S. military’s 58th strike against vessels it accused of smuggling drugs.
author: Eric Schmitt

title: Cubans Deported to Mexico Live a Precarious Existence, Report Finds
description: A Human Rights Watch report found that many Cubans deported to Mexico by the Trump administration are living in an “indefinite legal limbo” and struggling to get by.
author: Patricia Mazzei

title: Cornyn’s Defeat Fuels Tensions With President Trump in Senate G.O.P.
description: Senators are angry President Trump turned on a respected former leader whom they consider a loyal Republican. Now Mr. Trump faces resistance from his own embittered ranks.
author: Carl Hulse

title: 5 Things to Know About Christian Menefee, Democratic House Nominee in Texas
description: Mr. Menefee, one of the newest members of Congress, beat Representative Al Green, a Democrat who is more than twice his age, in a primary runoff.
author: Tim Balk

title: Evacuation Orders Lifted as Southern California Chemical Tank Cools
description: The risk of an explosion or spill has fallen significantly, the authorities said late Tuesday, and all evacuated residents can now go home. Some have begun to ask who should be held accountable.
author: Shawn Hubler and Rebecca Fairley Raney

title: Ken Paxton Victory Tests Whether Texas Republicans Can Reunite for November
description: Republican voters made a familiar bet, that Texas is conservative enough that any Republican, even the most conservative, will beat a Democrat.
author: J. David Goodman and Lauren McGaughy

title: Trump May Appear at N.B.A. Finals in New York
description: President Trump is said to be considering the appearance after his hometown team, the Knicks, clinched a championship spot.
author: Zolan Kanno-Youngs and Tyler Pager

title: Two of America’s Thorniest Political Issues Are Dividing an Arizona Town
description: Marana, Ariz., is used to placid local politics, but the town, in a congressional swing district held by Juan Ciscomani, has been convulsed by two national issues, A.I. and immigration detention.
author: Reis Thebault

title: Manny Fernandez, Bulwark of Dolphins’ Defense, Dies at 79
description: He played a critical part in Miami’s 17-0 season in 1972, which ended with his spectacular defense in Super Bowl VII against Washington.
author: Richard Sandomir

title: Endorsed by Trump, Paxton Crushes Cornyn: Takeaways From the Texas Runoff Elections
description: President Trump’s record of ousting those he sees as disloyal continued apace with Senator John Cornyn’s defeat. Whether his relationship with Senate Republicans can be repaired is another question.
author: Shane Goldmacher

title: Paxton’s Texas Victory Opens a New Front in the Battle for the Senate
description: Many Democrats and some Republicans said the scandal-plagued Ken Paxton’s victory could turn Texas into a battleground state that will determine Senate control.
author: Lisa Lerer and Reid J. Epstein

title: Ken Paxton Ousts John Cornyn, Solidifying Trump’s Grip on Republican Party Voters
description: Ken Paxton, the Texas attorney general, overcame scandals and a significant fund-raising disadvantage to win. His victory sets up the general-election clash that Democrats had hoped for.
author: Lauren McGaughy, J. David Goodman and Reid J. Epstein

title: Deaths Reported at Washington State Paper Mill After Chemical Tank Ruptures
description: Nine other people, including a firefighter, were injured at the Nippon Dynawave Packaging Company in Longview, the authorities said.
author: Christine Hauser, Mark Walker and Yan Zhuang
